summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a powerhouse 10‑9‑84 show – Help > Slip > Franklin’s, Jack Straw and He's Gone > Smokestack sparkle with stellar playing.
review: The recording is an exceptionally clean SBD mix that lets the band’s late‑summer energy shine.  The first set rocks hard while the second set builds from a soaring Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s into one of the era’s best Jack Straw and a fiery He's Gone → Smokestack, all backed by tight rhythm work and vivid keyboard textures.

ai_rating.rationale: Over twenty detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (standout jams, strong set flow) and the crystal‑clear soundboard source; only a few mixed notes keep the rating just shy of perfection.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp SBD mix with sharp snare crack, clear room ambience and minimal bleed.  Some reviewers note occasional audience patches, but overall the tape is described as perfect, sparkling and very percussive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is a high‑energy rock showcase; the second set launches from Help > Slip > Franklin’s into extended jams, delivering seamless transitions to Jack Straw and an inspired He's Gone → Smokestack.  The pacing stays engaging throughout, with the band swinging on every groove.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly praised – ‘trippy’, ‘awesome’, soaring solos on He’s Gone/Smokestack and a blistering lead on Jack Straw.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lukewarm to booming bass highlighted during the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence and a “reckoning bomb” after Let It Grow.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir gets kudos for slide work, vocal delivery on Jack Straw and overall rhythm guitar contribution.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) singled out for wild playing on Johnny B. Goode, clever delay effects on Throwing Stones, and a generally bright mix that sits well in the tape.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill & Mickey’s drumming noted for crackly snare, solid groove on Franklin’s Tower and driving power throughout the set.
